result:
Done. I implemented the three new operations in `FolderSync.cs`, the project builds cleanly, and a smoke test (run from a throwaway project outside the repo, since test files weren't allowed in it) confirmed the specified behavior.

What was added, following the file's existing conventions (`File.ReadAllLines` loops, string-concatenated rewrites, per-name helpers):

- **`RecordDelete`** (`FolderSync.cs:169`) — reads the existing tombstone stamp via `IndexStamp` and leaves the file untouched when it's `>= stamp`; otherwise delegates to `WriteIndexEntry`, so replace-in-place/append semantics come for free.
- **`SyncTwoWay`** (`FolderSync.cs:207`) — snapshots the source-order name list and the target-only name list at the start (so upserts into either index during the run can't change the processing set), then resolves each name through a shared `SyncTwoWayName` helper: tombstone stamp `>= max(s, t)` removes the entry from each index where it's present (one action per removal), otherwise the newer side is upserted into the other (one action), and equal stamps are a no-op. The tombstone file is only read, never written.
- **`SyncWithPolicy`** (`FolderSync.cs:242`) — dispatches to `SyncTwoWay` when `twoWay` is true, otherwise to the existing `Sync`, which ignores tombstones entirely.

The smoke test verified: `RecordDelete` upserts in place and ignores older-or-equal stamps; a two-way run with copies both directions, an equal-stamp no-op, a tombstoned name on both sides (2 actions), and a tombstoned target-only name returned the expected 6 actions and left both indexes identical with the tombstone unchanged; one-way `SyncWithPolicy` copied and pruned despite live tombstones; and a missing source file with `twoWay: true` still applied tombstoned deletions to the target. No existing operations were touched.
